headline: Samsung Electronics sees robust AI demand after Q1 profit surges eightfold to set record
summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit after an eightfold jump in earnings, saying it expects the momentum to continue as clients ‌spend aggressively on AI infrastructure and drive up prices of its memory chips.  A boom ‌in the construction of AI data centres has spurred chipmakers to allocate more production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia ​uses in its so-called AI accelerators, squeezing the supply of more conventional chips.  Samsung said it "expects server memory demand to remain strong as hyperscalers accommodate enterprises' increasing adoption of AI" and large language model services.

content.title: Samsung Electronics sees robust AI demand after Q1 profit surges eightfold to set record
content.summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit after an eightfold jump in earnings, saying it expects the momentum to continue as clients ‌spend aggressively on AI infrastructure and drive up prices of its memory chips.  A boom ‌in the construction of AI data centres has spurred chipmakers to allocate more production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia ​uses in its so-called AI accelerators, squeezing the supply of more conventional chips.  Samsung said it "expects server memory demand to remain strong as hyperscalers accommodate enterprises' increasing adoption of AI" and large language model services.

headline: Samsung chip profit jumps almost 50-fold; supply shortage to worsen in 2027
summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit driven by a 49-fold jump in chip income, saying it expects a severe supply shortage to deepen next year as clients spend on AI, driving up prices of its memory ‌chips.  A boom in the construction of AI data centres has spurred Samsung and chipmaking peers to allocate production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia uses in its so-called AI accelerators.  "Our supply falls far short of customer demand," Kim Jaejune, a Samsung memory chip business executive, told analysts on its post-earnings call.

content.title: Samsung chip profit jumps almost 50-fold; supply shortage to worsen in 2027
content.summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit driven by a 49-fold jump in chip income, saying it expects a severe supply shortage to deepen next year as clients spend on AI, driving up prices of its memory ‌chips.  A boom in the construction of AI data centres has spurred Samsung and chipmaking peers to allocate production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia uses in its so-called AI accelerators.  "Our supply falls far short of customer demand," Kim Jaejune, a Samsung memory chip business executive, told analysts on its post-earnings call.

headline: Exclusive-Prices of Nvidia's B300 server at $1 million in China on US curbs, sources say
summary: Strong demand for AI computing equipment in China has nearly doubled prices for Nvidia's B300 servers to about 7 million yuan ($1 million) each, industry sources said, as a crackdown ‌on chip smuggling dries up black-market supply.  Prices of Nvidia's most advanced and powerful server, critical for artificial intelligence tasks, have ‌climbed since early this year, but rose sharply after the grey market, a key supply channel, came under pressure, the four sources said.  The price surge is also being ​driven by robust computing demand from Chinese technology companies, even as many avoid holding Nvidia hardware directly on their books for fear of exposure to U.S. sanctions, the sources said.

content.title: Exclusive-Prices of Nvidia's B300 server at $1 million in China on US curbs, sources say
content.summary: Strong demand for AI computing equipment in China has nearly doubled prices for Nvidia's B300 servers to about 7 million yuan ($1 million) each, industry sources said, as a crackdown ‌on chip smuggling dries up black-market supply.  Prices of Nvidia's most advanced and powerful server, critical for artificial intelligence tasks, have ‌climbed since early this year, but rose sharply after the grey market, a key supply channel, came under pressure, the four sources said.  The price surge is also being ​driven by robust computing demand from Chinese technology companies, even as many avoid holding Nvidia hardware directly on their books for fear of exposure to U.S. sanctions, the sources said.

headline: Samsung chip profit jumps almost 50-fold; supply shortage to worsen
summary:
STORY: Samsung reported record quarterly profit on Thursday driven by a 49-fold jump in chip income.

The South Korean tech giant said January-March operating profit in its chip division reached a record $36.15 billion.

It was massively up from a year before and represented 94% of the quarter's total.

Overall revenue rose 69% on the year to $90.4 billion.

It marks a positive step for the firm which wants to close the gap with rival SK Hynix in supplying high bandwidth memory chips to Nvidia.

SK Hynix last week reported record quarterly profit following a fivefold jump in earnings.

The chipmakers have seen a boom in the construction of AI data centers.

It's led firms to allocate production capacity to advanced chips Nvidia uses in its so-called AI accelerators.

Chipmakers have struggled to meet demand while the move also squeezes the supply of conventional semiconductors.

Samsung said Thursday - quote - its supply falls far short of customer demand.

And it believes the supply-to-demand gap for 2027 is due to widen even further than this year.

The firm said continued development in AI tech will lead to sustained demand growth.

But supply will stay constrained for the time being due to the lead time required for new factory construction.

Elsewhere, Samsung said conflict in the Middle East hasn't disrupted chipmaking.

It cited secured inventory and diversified sources of gases used in manufacturing.

But it warned there's a risk of higher transportation costs caused by rising oil prices.

Samsung also faces possible production disruption as unions representing the majority of its workers in South Korea could strike over pay.

Samsung said it "plans to respond to the fullest extent to ensure production isn't disrupted."

Shares of the firm slipped almost 2.5% Thursday after rising as much as 1.8% following the earnings announcement.

The stock has surged 88% this year.

content.title: Samsung chip profit jumps almost 50-fold; supply shortage to worsen

headline: Up 3x, Has AMD Cracked The Nvidia Code?
summary: For most of the AI boom, Nvidia (NVDA) has been the only name that mattered in data center hardware. That dynamic is shifting. AMD (AMD) stock has risen 55% year-to-date and remains up 3.5x over the last 12 months, powered by record data center revenues and an increasingly competitive GPU lineup.

headline: Pony AI Expands Autonomous Suite As Investors Weigh Growth Versus Profitability Path
summary: Pony AI (NasdaqGS:PONY) announced a new generation autonomous driving domain controller developed with NVIDIA. The company expanded its Gen-7 Robotaxi platform with significant cost reductions and introduced a fully redundant L4 light-duty truck. Pony AI also unveiled a next generation AI world model for Virtual Driver training to support international commercialization across robotaxis and autonomous logistics. Pony AI operates in autonomous mobility, focusing on robotaxis and self driving...
